tex:
(a) Anvi buys a new car.
\newline
 % (a)
    (i) (b) The price of the car is $\$28\,240$.
\newline

        She is given a $7.5\%$ discount.
\newline

        Calculate the amount she pays.
\newline

        

        

(c) The fuel tank in the new car has a capacity of $45$ litres.
\newline

        This is $72\%$ of the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
\newline

        

        Calculate the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
\newline

        

        

(d) Aadi buys a new car costing $\$28\,000$.
\newline
 % (b)

    He pays for the car using a finance plan.
\newline

    The finance plan is
    (e) a deposit

(f) 47 equal monthly payments of $\$330$

(g) a final payment of $\$11\,490$.
\newline
    Using this finance plan, Aadi pays a total of $\$31\,900$ for the car.
\newline

        
    Calculate the deposit paid as a percentage of $\$28\,000$.
\newline

    

    

(h) A car travels $64$ km and uses $2.5$ litres of fuel.
\newline
 % (c)

    It then travels $128$ km and uses $6$ litres of fuel.
\newline

        
    Calculate the rate at which the car uses fuel during the whole journey.
\newline

    Give your answer in litres per $100$ km.
\newline

        

    

(i) At the start of 2021 the value of a car was $\$46\,500$.
\newline
 % (d)

    At the end of 2021 the value of the car was $20\%$ less.
\newline

    At the end of 2022 the value of the car was $15\%$ less than its value at the end of 2021.
\newline

        
    Calculate the value of the car at the end of 2022.
\newline

content.content:
Anvi buys a new car.
\newline
 % (a)

The price of the car is $\$28\,240$.
\newline

        She is given a $7.5\%$ discount.
\newline

Calculate the amount she pays.
\newline

The fuel tank in the new car has a capacity of $45$ litres.
\newline

        This is $72\%$ of the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
\newline

Calculate the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
\newline

Aadi buys a new car costing $\$28\,000$.
\newline
 % (b)

    He pays for the car using a finance plan.
\newline

    The finance plan is
    \begin{itemize}
a deposit
47 equal monthly payments of $\$330$

a final payment of $\$11\,490$.
\newline
    \end{itemize}

    Using this finance plan, Aadi pays a total of $\$31\,900$ for the car.
\newline

Calculate the deposit paid as a percentage of $\$28\,000$.
\newline

A car travels $64$ km and uses $2.5$ litres of fuel.
\newline
 % (c)

    It then travels $128$ km and uses $6$ litres of fuel.
\newline

Calculate the rate at which the car uses fuel during the whole journey.
\newline

    Give your answer in litres per $100$ km.
\newline

At the start of 2021 the value of a car was $\$46\,500$.
\newline
 % (d)

    At the end of 2021 the value of the car was $20\%$ less.
\newline

    At the end of 2022 the value of the car was $15\%$ less than its value at the end of 2021.
\newline

Calculate the value of the car at the end of 2022.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
    \item Anvi buys a new car. % (a)
    \begin{subparts}
        \item The price of the car is $\$28\,240$.

        She is given a $7.5\%$ discount.

        \vgap

        Calculate the amount she pays.
        
        \vgap
        
        \AnswerLine[$\$$][]
        \Marks{2}

        \item The fuel tank in the new car has a capacity of $45$ litres.

        This is $72\%$ of the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
        
        \vgap
        
        Calculate the capacity of the fuel tank in her old car.
        
        \vgap
        
        \AnswerLine[][litres]
        \Marks{2}
    \end{subparts}

    \item Aadi buys a new car costing $\$28\,000$. % (b)

    He pays for the car using a finance plan.

    The finance plan is
    \begin{itemize}
        \item a deposit
        \item 47 equal monthly payments of $\$330$
        \item a final payment of $\$11\,490$.
    \end{itemize}

    Using this finance plan, Aadi pays a total of $\$31\,900$ for the car.

    \vgap
        
    Calculate the deposit paid as a percentage of $\$28\,000$.
    
    \vgap
        
    \AnswerLine[][\%]
    \Marks{4}

    \item A car travels $64$ km and uses $2.5$ litres of fuel. % (c)

    It then travels $128$ km and uses $6$ litres of fuel.

    \vgap
        
    Calculate the rate at which the car uses fuel during the whole journey.

    Give your answer in litres per $100$ km.

    \vgap
        
    \AnswerLine[][litres per $100$ km]
    \Marks{2}

    \item At the start of 2021 the value of a car was $\$46\,500$. % (d)

    At the end of 2021 the value of the car was $20\%$ less.

    At the end of 2022 the value of the car was $15\%$ less than its value at the end of 2021.

    \vgap
        
    Calculate the value of the car at the end of 2022.

    \vgap
        
    \AnswerLine[$\$$][]
    \Marks{2}
\end{parts}

qtype: drawing cubic graphs, drawing tangents, estimating gradient, solving equations graphically
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = x^3 + 4x^2 - 4$.
\newline

(a) Complete the table.
\newline
 

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^3 + 4x^2 - 4$ for $-4.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 1.5$.
\newline

(c) % (c)
    (i) (d) Draw the tangent to the graph at the point $(1, 1)$.
\newline

(e) Use your tangent to estimate the gradient of the curve at the point $(1, 1)$.
\newline

        

(f) By drawing a suitable straight line on the grid, solve the equation $x^3 + 4x^2 - x - 6 = 0$.
\newline

      or  or

content.content:
Complete the table.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^3 + 4x^2 - 4$ for $-4.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 1.5$.
\newline

Draw the tangent to the graph at the point $(1, 1)$.
\newline

Use your tangent to estimate the gradient of the curve at the point $(1, 1)$.
\newline

By drawing a suitable straight line on the grid, solve the equation $x^3 + 4x^2 - x - 6 = 0$.
\newline

stem.content:
The table shows some values for $y = x^3 + 4x^2 - 4$.
\newline

qtype: algebraic simplification, indices, algebraic fractions, forming equations, solving quadratic and simultaneous equations
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline
 % (a)
    (i) (b) $3m - 5n - 4m + 8n$

        

(c) $(3a^2 c^3)^4$

        

(d) $\dfrac{4x}{5} - \dfrac{3x}{10} + \dfrac{2x}{15}$

        

(e) This isosceles triangle has a perimeter of $35.5$ cm.
\newline
 % (b)

    Find the value of $a$.
\newline

    

(f) Using the quadratic formula, solve $5x^2 - 4x - 3 = 0$.

    You must show all your working.
\newline

      or 

(g) Solve these simultaneous equations.
\newline
 % (d)
    \[ y = x^2 - 4x + 5 \]
    \[ y = 2x - 3 \]

    You must show all your working.
\newline

     , $y = $

     , $y = $

tex:
(a) The angles of a quadrilateral are $w^\circ, x^\circ, y^\circ$ and $z^\circ$.
\newline
 % (a)

    The ratio $w : (x + y + z) = 3 : 5$.
\newline

    Find the value of $w$.
\newline

    

(b)  % (b)

    $A, B, C$ and $D$ are points on a circle.
\newline

    $PQ$ is the tangent to the circle at $A$.
\newline

    $BMND$ is a straight line.
\newline

    Angle $ACD = 49^\circ$, angle $AMB = 105^\circ$ and angle $PAB = 45^\circ$.
\newline
    (i) (c) Find angle $BAM$.
\newline

        

(d) % (ii)
        \begin{subsubparts}

(e) Find angle $BAD$.
\newline

            

(f) Give a geometrical reason why $BD$ is not the diameter of the circle.
\newline

            

            ...

            

            ...

        \end{subsubparts}

(g)  % (c)

    $A, B, C$ and $D$ are points on a circle, centre $O$.
\newline

    $TA$ and $TC$ are tangents to the circle.
\newline

    $OA = 6.75$ cm and $OT = 11.5$ cm.
\newline
    (i) (h) Show that angle $AOC = 108.12^\circ$, correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

        

(i) Calculate the length of the minor arc $ABC$.
\newline

        

(j) Calculate the area of the major sector $OCDA$.
\newline

 % (a)

\newline

Find the value of $w$.
\newline

$A, B, C$ and $D$ are points on a circle.
\newline

    $PQ$ is the tangent to the circle at $A$.
\newline

    $BMND$ is a straight line.
\newline

    Angle $ACD = 49^\circ$, angle $AMB = 105^\circ$ and angle $PAB = 45^\circ$.
\newline

Find angle $BAM$.
\newline
% (ii)
Find angle $BAD$.
Give a geometrical reason why $BD$ is not the diameter of the circle.

$A, B, C$ and $D$ are points on a circle, centre $O$.
\newline

    $TA$ and $TC$ are tangents to the circle.
\newline

    $OA = 6.75$ cm and $OT = 11.5$ cm.
\newline

Show that angle $AOC = 108.12^\circ$, correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

Calculate the length of the minor arc $ABC$.
\newline

Calculate the area of the major sector $OCDA$.
\newline

tex:
(a) The cumulative frequency diagram shows information about the distance travelled by each of 80 motorists in a month.
\newline
 % (a)

    (i) (b) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate for
        \begin{subsubparts}

(c) the median

            

(d) the interquartile range

            
        \end{subsubparts}

(e) One of these motorists is picked at random.
\newline

        Find the probability that this motorist travels more than $1800$ km.
\newline

        

(f) The distance around a racing track is $5.104$ km.
\newline
 % (b)

    The time taken by a car to complete one lap of the track is $1$ min $18$ s.
\newline

    Calculate the average speed of the car.
\newline

    Give your answer in km/h.
\newline

    

(g) The top speed, $v$ km/h, of each of 160 cars is recorded.
\newline
 % (c)

    The histogram shows this information.
\newline

    (i) (h) Show that there are 8 cars with a top speed in the interval $120 < v \leqslant 160$.
\newline

(i) Calculate an estimate of the mean top speed.
\newline

        You must show all your working.
\newline

 % (a)

the interquartile range

Calculate an estimate of the mean top speed.
\newline

        You must show all your working.

tex:
(a) Work out $2\dbinom{3}{-5} - \dbinom{-2}{-7}$.
\newline
 % (a)
     $\dbinom{}{}$

(b) $\overrightarrow{MN} = \dbinom{-6}{4}$.
\newline
 % (b)
    (i) (c) $M$ is the point $(2, -5)$.
\newline

        Find the coordinates of $N$.
\newline

         (, )

(d) Find $|\overrightarrow{MN}|$.
\newline

        

(e)  % (c)

    $OACB$ is a trapezium with $OB = 2AC$.
\newline

    $\overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{OB} = 2\mathbf{c}$.
\newline

    $AP : PB = 4 : 1$ and $AQ = \dfrac{4}{5}AC$.
\newline
    (i) (f) Write each of the following in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{c}$.
\newline

        Give each answer in its simplest form.
\newline
        \begin{subsubparts}

(g) $\overrightarrow{AB}$

            

(h) $\overrightarrow{CB}$

            

(i) $\overrightarrow{OP}$

            

(j) $\overrightarrow{QP}$

            
        \end{subsubparts}

(k) Use your answers to make two statements about the relationship between lines $QP$ and $CB$.
\newline

        ...

        ...

Work out $2\dbinom{3}{-5} - \dbinom{-2}{-7}$.
\newline

    \hfill $\dbinom{\hspace{0.5cm}}{\hspace{0.5cm}}$

$\overrightarrow{MN} = \dbinom{-6}{4}$.
\newline
 % (b)

$M$ is the point $(2, -5)$.
\newline

        Find the coordinates of $N$.
\newline

Find $|\overrightarrow{MN}|$.
\newline

$OACB$ is a trapezium with $OB = 2AC$.
\newline

    $\overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{OB} = 2\mathbf{c}$.
\newline

    $AP : PB = 4 : 1$ and $AQ = \dfrac{4}{5}AC$.
\newline

Write each of the following in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{c}$.
\newline

        Give each answer in its simplest form.
\newline
$\overrightarrow{AB}$
$\overrightarrow{CB}$
$\overrightarrow{OP}$
$\overrightarrow{QP}$

Use your answers to make two statements about the relationship between lines $QP$ and $CB$.
\newline

tex:
(a)  % (a)

    The diagram shows a field, $ABCD$ with $B$ north of $A$.
\newline

    $BD$ is a path across the field.
\newline

    $AB = 85$ m, $AD = 72$ m, $BD = 129$ m, angle $BDC = 39^\circ$ and angle $BCD = 60^\circ$.
\newline
    (i) (b) Show that angle $CBD = 81^\circ$.
\newline

(c) Calculate $CD$.
\newline

        

(d) Show that angle $ABD = 31.6^\circ$, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

(e) Find the shortest distance from $A$ to $BD$.
\newline

        

(f) Find the bearing of $B$ from $C$.
\newline

        

(g) Trees are planted in the field.
\newline

        The number of trees planted is 1100 per hectare.
\newline

        Calculate the total number of trees planted in the field.
\newline

        $[1 \text{ hectare} = 10\,000 \text{ m}^2]$

        

(h) A rectangle has an area of $9400 \text{ cm}^2$, correct to the nearest $100 \text{ cm}^2$.
\newline
 % (b)

    The length of the rectangle is $80$ cm, correct to the nearest $10$ cm.
\newline

    
    Calculate the upper bound of the width of the rectangle.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

        The number of trees planted is 1100 per hectare.
\newline

Calculate the total number of trees planted in the field.
\newline

        $[1 \text{ hectare} = 10\,000 \text{ m}^2]$

A rectangle has an area of $9400 \text{ cm}^2$, correct to the nearest $100 \text{ cm}^2$.
\newline
 % (b)

    The length of the rectangle is $80$ cm, correct to the nearest $10$ cm.

Calculate the upper bound of the width of the rectangle.
\newline

tex:
(a) A bag contains 24 coloured beads.
\newline
 % (a)

    Some are red, some are blue and 10 are yellow.
\newline

    One bead is picked at random from the bag.
\newline

    Find the probability that
    (i) (b) the bead is yellow

        

(c) the bead is not yellow.
\newline

        

(d) Another bag contains 5 green marbles, 6 white marbles and 4 black marbles.
\newline
 % (b)

    Meera picks 2 marbles at random from the bag, without replacement.
\newline

    
    Find the probability that
    (i) (e) the first marble is black and the second marble is white

        

(f) both marbles have different colours.
\newline

content.content:
A bag contains 24 coloured beads.
\newline
 % (a)

    Some are red, some are blue and 10 are yellow.
\newline

    One bead is picked at random from the bag.
\newline
Find the probability that
the bead is yellow

the bead is not yellow.
\newline

Another bag contains 5 green marbles, 6 white marbles and 4 black marbles.
\newline
 % (b)

    Meera picks 2 marbles at random from the bag, without replacement.
\newline
Find the probability that
the first marble is black and the second marble is white

both marbles have different colours.
\newline

qtype: function evaluation, composite functions, inverse functions, algebraic manipulation of functions
tex:
$f(x) = 2x - 5$ \qquad $g(x) = x^2 - 2x$

(a) Find % (a)
    (i) (b) $f(7)$

        

(c) $gf(7)$

        

(d) $f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

        

(e) Find $gf(x) - 3g(x)$.
\newline
 \\ % (b)
    Give your answer in the form $ax^2 + bx + c$.
\newline

content.content: Find % (a)

$f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

Find $gf(x) - 3g(x)$.
\newline
 \\ % (b)
    Give your answer in the form $ax^2 + bx + c$.
\newline

qtype: differentiation, finding turning points, determining nature of turning points
tex:
A curve has the equation $y = x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline

(a) Differentiate $x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline
 % (a)

    

(b) Find the coordinates of the turning points of the graph of $y = x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline
 

    You must show all your working.
\newline

     (,) and (,)

(c) Determine whether each of the turning points is a maximum or a minimum.
\newline
 

    Give reasons for your answers.
\newline

content.content:
Differentiate $x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline
 % (a)

Find the coordinates of the turning points of the graph of $y = x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline
 
    
    You must show all your working.
\newline

Determine whether each of the turning points is a maximum or a minimum.
\newline
 
    
    Give reasons for your answers.
\newline

stem.content:
A curve has the equation $y = x^3 - 9x^2 - 48x$.
\newline
